mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through higher corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved corporate earnings.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during monetary tightening phases, producing negative 5-day returns in >50% of cases when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and high consumer price index levels creates a restrictive real rate environment that suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive when the interaction term is active, or the mechanism shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and consumer base, directly supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows, or positive payroll growth coincides with negative equity returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Real gross domestic product growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms economic expansion and rising corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of triggered quarters, or loses significance when adjusted for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a synchronized economic upturn that boosts market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive returns when housing starts decline simultaneously, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.
